text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE

Clinic Date: 08 June 2020

Dr Michael Shah
Southgate Health Centre

42 High Street, Southgate, London N14 6EB

Dear Dr Michael Shah,

Daniel Harris, DOB: 14-08-1986, Hospital No: H937420, NHS No: 8412097563
Flat 3, 15 Brookfield Road, London N16 0AH

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent seizures of uncertain character; prior normal MRI (2006) and multiple EEG/telemetry assessments without a definitive focus identified. No structural cause established.

Background and current status:
I reviewed Daniel Harris in clinic today, with information supplemented by his seizure diary app, which he has been using consistently. The diary entries provide time-stamped logs of episodes, associated symptoms, and any rescue or regular medication use.

Seizure pattern from diary review:
After experiencing dizziness and headache on two occasions, his symptoms improved without complications. On 07-Mar his absences settled with treatment. The most recent episode was on 15-Mar, and since then he has been well. With no recurrence for the past months and overall stable epilepsy, no additional management is indicated at this stage.

Medications:
- Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily (well tolerated; no rash, no dose changes in the last 6 months)
- Folic acid 5 mg once daily
- Metoclopramide as required for nausea

Previously tried/ceased (patient-reported, prior years): levetiracetam (tiredness and irritability), clobazam (intermittent use), carbamazepine, valproate, phenytoin, phenobarbital, gabapentin, vigabatrin, lacosamide. No current rescue benzodiazepine use recorded in the app. No history of topiramate, zonisamide, pregabalin, tiagabine, acetazolamide, perampanel, retigabine, rufinamide in recent years per diary; patient not wishing to trial new agents while stable.

Investigations (historical):
- MRI brain 2006: No definite epileptogenic lesion reported.
- Prior EEG/telemetry on more than one occasion: no consistent localising features captured.

Functional impact and wellbeing:
He reports good sleep hygiene and regular hydration. No recent injuries, hospital attendances, or medication side effects. He continues to use the diary app, which is helpful in identifying potential triggers; none consistent in the last quarter.

Plan:
- Continue current regimen unchanged, given stability and absence of recurrent events.
- Continue seizure diary app logging; advised to include medication timing and any intercurrent illness.
- Routine follow-up in 6 months via clinic, with earlier review if episodes recur or if any adverse effects emerge.
- Safety advice reissued today (driving, bathing unsupervised, working at heights) as per national guidance; patient confirms understanding.

I will be happy to review sooner if there is any change in his condition.

Yours sincerely,

After experiencing dizziness and headache on two occasions, his symptoms improved without complications. On 07-Mar his absences settled with treatment. The most recent episode was on 15-Mar, and since then he has been well. With no recurrence for the past months and overall stable epilepsy, no additional management is indicated at this stage

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis:
- The letter explicitly states: "The most recent episode was on 15-Mar, and since then he has been well."
- Clinic date is 08 June 2020. Time from 15-Mar to 08-Jun ≈ 2 months 24 days, i.e., about 3 months.
- He has not been seizure-free for at least 6 months, so cannot be labeled seizure free per the rules.
- With one documented episode in the roughly 3-month interval up to the clinic date and none since, the inferred current frequency is 1 per 3 month.
seizure_frequency_number: The most recent episode was on 15-Mar, and since then he has been well.
